issues.comment: ERB syntax error: `end if` instead of `end`. In Ruby, `end if` is a conditional modifier that requires a boolean expression after `if`. Here `end if` appears with no condition, making it a syntax error that will crash the template on every render. The correct code is `<% end %>`.

Origin validation bypass via `indexOf`. The check `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1` tests whether `e.origin` is a substring of `discourseUrl`, which is backwards and insecure. An attacker's origin like `http://example.co` would match as a prefix of `http://example.com/embed/`, bypassing the origin check. This allows an attacker on a prefix-matching domain to send arbitrary `postMessage` events. The correct approach is to compare the origin portion of `discourseUrl` strictly, e.g., `if (e.origin !== new URL(discourseUrl).origin) { return; }`.

`X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL` is not a valid value per the HTTP spec. Valid values are `DENY`, `SAMEORIGIN`, and `ALLOW-FROM uri`. Browser behavior with `ALLOWALL` is undefined — some may ignore the header entirely (desired), others may treat it as `DENY` (breaking embedding). The fix is to delete the header entirely: `response.headers.delete("X-Frame-Options")`.

`NoMethodError` when RSS item has no `content` field. Many RSS feeds use `<description>` rather than `<content>` elements. When an RSS item lacks a `content` field, `i.content` returns `nil` in SimpleRSS, and calling `.scrub` on `nil` raises `NoMethodError: undefined method 'scrub' for nil:NilClass`. This will crash the entire feed polling job. Should fall back to `i.description` or guard against nil, e.g., `i.content || i.description || ""`.

SSRF via `open()` with user-controlled URL. `Kernel#open` (via `open-uri`) fetches arbitrary URLs including `file://` schemes in some Ruby versions and internal network addresses. In `import_remote`, the `url` comes from external content (e.g., Disqus link data), enabling SSRF to probe internal services. The same issue exists in `app/jobs/scheduled/poll_feed.rb` line 28. Should use a safe URL fetcher that validates URIs and blocks internal IPs.
